title: Show connected providers and default models in listing
description: Use client.provider.list() to cross-reference connected providers with the default model map. --list-providers should only show connected providers (from the connected array) and mark which model is the default for each. --list-models should mark the default model for each connected provider. The default map from provider.list() covers all known providers; filter to only connected ones. This replaces the current approach of using config.providers() which lacks connection status.

title: Pass existing summary markdown into new session
description:
When starting a new session (either explicitly or because context limit was reached), pass the current summary markdown file content as context at the beginning of the session. This way the LLM has continuity with what's already been written — it knows the style, format, and prior chapter summaries even across session boundaries.

Works in conjunction with ps-markdown-output: if --output is specified, read the existing file and include it as a preamble in the first prompt of a new session.

title: Append summaries to markdown file instead of stdout
description:
Add a --output flag that specifies a markdown file to append summaries to. Preferably pass the filename to opencode as a resource/context so the LLM knows it's writing into an existing document and can format accordingly (headers, continuity with prior content).

If we can pass the file as a resource via the opencode SDK (file attachment or path context), do that. Otherwise, at minimum capture the LLM output and append it to the specified markdown file with a chapter heading.

title: Re-use single session across multiple chapters
description:
Support passing multiple chapters on the command line (e.g. -c 5,6,7 or -c 5-8 or -c 5 -c 6 -c 7). Re-use a single opencode session for all chapters, sending each chapter's content sequentially so the LLM has context from prior chapter summaries. This gives better continuity — each summary can reference earlier chapters.

Also add a --context-usage flag that prints token usage (input/output/cache) after each chapter is summarized, so the user can monitor context window consumption.

Also add a --context-limit flag (accepts either a percentage like '80%' or an absolute token count like '100000') that controls when to automatically start a new session if the context window approaches the limit. This prevents hitting the model's max context and getting truncated responses.

title: Select provider and model via CLI flags
description: Support --provider and --model flags to choose which provider and model to use for the opencode session. Currently the model plugin registers these flags but selection should validate the choice exists in the provider list, show a friendly error if not found, and persist the selection for the session. Consider also supporting a default config so users don't have to pass flags every time.

title: List providers and models with current default indicator
description: When using --list-providers and --list-models, indicate which provider/model is the current default. The default comes from config.get() model field or from the provider list's default map. Highlight or mark the default row in the output table so the user knows what will be used if no --provider/--model is specified.

title: mDNS address finding for opencode service
description: Support discovering opencode service address via mDNS (multicast DNS). The plugin should browse for service records, pick a random one from the results, and use that as the baseUrl for the opencode SDK client. This replaces the hardcoded localhost:4096 address.
